Dockerfile.general_idea: Refactor Dockerfile structure by removing manual package installation and directory creation, relying instead on the new base image and simplified copy commands.
Dockerfile.before:
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl

ENV JAVA_OPTS ""

RUN mkdir -p /opt/keycloak-config-cli/configs

Dockerfile.general_idea: Change the working directory and volume mount path to a new location (/tmp/keycloak-config-cli) as part of the container structure refactor.
Simplify artifact copying by using a fixed filename and copying additional root files, removing specific script copies.
Change the container entrypoint from a custom bash script to a generic bash shell, likely delegating startup logic to the new base image or external orchestration.

docker-compose.yml.general_idea: Remove the explicit PostgreSQL service definition from the compose file.
Upgrade the Keycloak container image from version 3.2.1 to 9.0.0 to support Java 11 and new features.
Remove legacy environment variables for external PostgreSQL configuration as the database service is removed.
docker-compose.yml.before:
POSTGRES_USER: keycloak
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: keycloak
      POSTGRES_PORT_5432_TCP_ADDR: postgres

docker-compose.yml.general_idea: Remove legacy volume mounts for Keycloak data persistence.
Remove legacy 'links' directive for service discovery in favor of Docker Compose networking.
Update the config-cli volume mount path to match the new project structure.
Add new environment variables for the config-cli service to control import behavior and debugging.
Add an explicit command to the config-cli service to ensure it runs the configuration tool.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update maven-jar-plugin to version 3.1.2 to ensure compatibility with Java 11 and modern Maven standards.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-jar-plugin may not correctly handle Java 11 module descriptors or manifest entries, potentially causing runtime issues.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add maven-shade-plugin to support creating fat JARs in the new module structure.
pom.xml.why_exists: The previous setup relied on Spring Boot's repackaging; the new structure may require explicit shading for certain modules or dependencies.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add jacoco-maven-plugin for code coverage reporting in the new build structure.
pom.xml.why_exists: Code coverage was likely handled differently or not at all in the previous setup; this adds explicit coverage support.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add docker-maven-plugin to support containerized builds in the new environment.
pom.xml.why_exists: The previous setup did not include explicit Docker build support in the POM; this adds it for CI/CD integration.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add buildnumber-maven-plugin to include build metadata in the JAR manifest.
pom.xml.why_exists: This provides traceability for builds, which is a best practice but not strictly required for compilation.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add distributionManagement to publish artifacts to GitHub Packages.
pom.xml.why_exists: This enables CI/CD deployment to a new repository, replacing or supplementing previous distribution methods.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add project metadata (licenses, developers, SCM) to the POM for better artifact documentation.
pom.xml.why_exists: This improves artifact quality and compliance but is not required for compilation or runtime.

pom.xml.general_idea: Remove the ConfigurationApp profile and exec-maven-plugin configuration from the root POM.
pom.xml.why_exists: The execution of the main class is now likely handled by the child module or a different mechanism, making the root profile obsolete.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add hibernate-validator dependency to dependencyManagement to ensure consistent versioning across modules.
pom.xml.why_exists: Hibernate Validator 6.1.0.Final is required for compatibility with newer Spring Boot and Java 11 features.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add test dependencies (hamcrest, catch-exception, maven-artifact) to dependencyManagement.
pom.xml.why_exists: These dependencies are needed for testing in the new module structure but were not explicitly managed in the root POM before.
